objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 assignment workflow assessment where a student can submit work as another student by changing the object owner.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real external target is contacted.
Students, assignments, and submissions are intentionally fake.

actions: Load the synthetic baseline submission for the acting student
Identify whether the server derives student ownership from the authenticated session or trusts request body fields
successCriteria: Baseline ownership behavior is recorded as durable evidence
noSubagents: student-impact and disclosure boundaries stay with the primary operator

actions: Submit a synthetic assignment as student-4401 while setting studentId to student-4402
Fetch the target student's submission to confirm state mutation
Record the missing object-owner authorization control
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites both the forged submit request and resulting target-student submission state
noSubagents: final exploitability and remediation wording stay with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic forged assignment submission accepted
evidence.summary: A student-authenticated request submitted assignment text for another synthetic student by changing the studentId field.

evidence.title: Synthetic target-student submission changed
evidence.summary: The target student's assignment record reflected the attacker-controlled submission text after the forged request.

findings.title: Assignment submission endpoint trusts client-supplied student ownership
findings.description: The synthetic assignment submission endpoint accepts studentId from the request body and writes to that student's assignment record without confirming it matches the authenticated student.
findings.impact: A student account could overwrite or submit work for another student, creating academic integrity, disciplinary, and student-support workflow risk.
findings.remediation: Derive student ownership from the authenticated session, reject body-supplied owner fields for student submissions, enforce assignment access checks server-side, and audit attempts to submit for another student.
